This research unlocks faster deployment for diffusion-based AI, offering a practical speedup mechanism for latency-sensitive applications where performance and reliability are critical.

name: Yihan Wang
paper_title: WAFT: Warping-Alone Field Transforms for Optical Flow
synopsis: This research addresses the high computational cost and memory usage of optical flow estimation, which currently relies on expensive mechanisms that limit speed and resolution in autonomous driving applications. The authors introduce WAFT, a streamlined architecture that replaces these heavy components with memory-efficient warping techniques to achieve faster processing without sacrificing accuracy. Experimental results show WAFT ranks first on key benchmarks like KITTI and Spring while running up to 4.1 times faster than existing high-performing methods with competitive accuracy. This efficiency makes the technology viable for scalable real-world deployments in self-driving vehicles, video frame interpolation, and consumer computer vision tools where lower latency is critical.

name: Xinyang Li
paper_title: FlashWorld: High-quality 3D Scene Generation within Seconds
synopsis: This research addresses the bottleneck of creating high-quality full 3D scenes efficiently, which currently suffers from significant latency or visual inconsistencies compared to generating single objects. The authors introduce FlashWorld, a model that uses a dual-mode training strategy to combine the fidelity of multi-view generation with the consistency of direct 3D representations, refined further by cross-mode distillation to ensure superior quality without slowing down inference. Experimental results demonstrate that the system generates realistic 3D environments in seconds, improving speed and visual clarity by 10 to 100 times compared to existing state-of-the-art pipelines. This technology unlocks scalable workflows for high-demand industries such as gaming, robotics, and virtual reality where rapid production of immersive assets is required.

name: Xiangjian Jiang
paper_title: TabStruct: Measuring Structural Fidelity of Tabular Data
synopsis: Evaluating tabular generative models has long been flawed because existing benchmarks focus on predictive performance rather than verifying if synthetic data respects the underlying causal structures of real-world data. This paper addresses that gap by introducing TabStruct, a comprehensive framework featuring a new global utility metric capable of measuring structural fidelity without requiring ground-truth causal graphs. Their findings show that models scoring well on conventional metrics often fail to obey the causal laws inherent in real-world systems, indicating high risks in current deployment strategies. Consequently, this research offers enterprises a reliable validation tool for tasks like missing value imputation and data augmentation, ensuring synthetic data remains logically consistent in sensitive sectors like healthcare or finance.

name: Haochen Zhou
paper_title: Uncover Underlying Correspondence for Robust Multi-view Clustering
synopsis: This paper addresses the critical issue of noisy data pairs that disrupt multi-view clustering by treating samples from the same category as mismatched. The researchers propose CorreGen, a generative framework that uses an Expectation-Maximization algorithm to mathematically infer true correspondences and filter out unreliable connections without needing costly manual cleaning. Their approach significantly boosts clustering performance on web-scraped datasets with up to 10% accuracy improvement over previous methods in high-noise scenarios. This technology enables more robust analytics for industries relying on imperfect cross-modal data, such as automated image-text search and surveillance systems where perfect alignment is unfeasible.

name: Jianwen Jiang
paper_title: Instilling an Active Mind in Avatars via Cognitive Simulation
synopsis: Current video avatar models often fail to capture authentic emotion or intent because they rely on reactive audio cues rather than understanding higher-level semantics. This paper introduces a framework that integrates Multimodal Large Language Models to generate high-level reasoning alongside specialized diffusion architectures for motion synthesis. By using a novel design to fuse text guidance with audio and visual signals, the model achieves superior video quality and semantic consistency even in complex multi-person scenarios. These capabilities enable the creation of lifelike digital humans capable of reasoned action, which will significantly enhance applications in virtual meetings, immersive gaming, and advanced metaverse experiences.

Empirical evaluations demonstrate that this approach reduces the number of required decoding steps by up to 3.4× while maintaining high generation quality without additional training costs. This research unlocks faster deployment for diffusion-based AI, offering a practical speedup mechanism for latency-sensitive applications where performance and reliability are critical.

name: Xiangjun Tang
institution: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
paper_title: Generative Human Geometry Distribution
synopsis: This paper addresses the challenge of generating realistic human geometry that preserves fine clothing details and body interactions while remaining scalable for large datasets. The authors propose a novel framework that encodes geometry distributions as 2D feature maps and utilizes SMPL templates to optimize flow matching for efficient learning. Their two-stage training paradigm achieves a significant 57% improvement in geometry quality over current state-of-the-art techniques, proving superior fidelity without sacrificing speed. These advancements enable practical applications like generating pose-conditioned avatars and ensuring consistent character rendering across novel poses for virtual try-on platforms and interactive environments.

name: Dingdong WANG
institution: Chinese University of Hong Kong, The Chinese University of Hong Kong
paper_title: EmotionThinker: Prosody-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Explainable Speech Emotion Reasoning
synopsis: Current speech large language models treat emotion detection as simple classification tasks, failing to explain the reasoning behind their predictions or fully leverage critical acoustic prosody like pitch and intonation. To address this, EmotionThinker introduces a three-stage framework that curates a fine-grained Chain-of-Thought dataset and utilizes reinforcement learning to reward high-quality reasoning aligned with acoustic evidence. The resulting system significantly outperforms existing benchmarks by combining strong prosody perception with interpretability, allowing models to justify emotional judgments based on speaker traits and tone. This capability advances applications in human-computer interaction and affective computing, enabling AI systems to understand nuanced user sentiment rather than merely categorizing it for more trustworthy and responsive interactions.

name: Yaoyu Wang
institution: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
paper_title: Mastering Sparse CUDA Generation through Pretrained Models and Deep Reinforcement Learning
synopsis: This paper addresses the challenge of automatically generating high-performance CUDA code for sparse matrix operations, which is essential for low-latency applications like AI inference but difficult due to irregular data structures. The authors propose SparseRL, a framework that combines pretrained language models with deep reinforcement learning to optimize code specifically for execution efficiency and correctness based on dynamic input structures. They achieve this by embedding sparse row/column indices directly and using a hierarchical reward function that penalizes both compilation failures and slow runtime performance. Experimental results show the method improves compilation rates by 20% and accelerates generated code by 30%, providing a scalable solution for graph neural networks, pruned large language models, and other high-performance computing tasks.

name: Yuhao Wu
institution: Singapore University of Technology and Design
paper_title: LongWriter-Zero: Mastering Ultra-Long Text Generation via Reinforcement Learning
synopsis: LongWriter-Zero addresses the challenge of generating ultra-long text where large language models typically degrade in quality or rely on costly synthetic datasets. The researchers overcome this by using reinforcement learning to train a model from scratch with specialized reward signals that ensure coherence, appropriate length, and structural formatting. Experimental results demonstrate that this RL-based approach consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art models, including those with much larger parameters, on long-form writing benchmarks. This breakthrough enables reliable practical use cases such as multi-section legal reports, in-depth narrative storytelling, and comprehensive educational content creation without manual data curation.

name: Peiyan Hu
paper_title: RealPDEBench: A Benchmark for Complex Physical Systems with Real-World Data
synopsis: This research tackles the critical gap in scientific machine learning where models trained on simulated data frequently fail when deployed in real-world physical environments due to measurement noise and simplified physics. To solve this, the authors present RealPDEBench, a novel benchmark featuring paired real-world measurements and numerical simulations across five complex systems like fluid dynamics and combustion. Experimental results show that while discrepancies between simulation and reality are significant, pretraining models on simulated data before fine-tuning with actual experiments consistently improves prediction accuracy. Ultimately, this framework enables reliable AI deployment in engineering by bridging the sim-to-real gap and validating foundation models against high-cost experimental data.

name: Feng Hong
paper_title: Improving Diffusion Models for Class-imbalanced Training Data via Capacity Manipulation
synopsis: This study tackles a critical limitation in AI image generation where diffusion models fail on rare categories because dominant data classes consume excessive model capacity. To fix this, the researchers introduce Capacity Manipulation, a technique that restructures neural network parameters to explicitly reserve resources for minority classes without requiring larger models or more compute. Extensive testing proves this method consistently boosts performance on underrepresented groups while maintaining high quality for common classes, offering a plug-and-play upgrade to current tools. This innovation is particularly valuable for industries facing data scarcity, such as healthcare diagnostics or fraud detection, ensuring reliable generation of rare but important samples.

name: Junsong Chen
paper_title: SANA-Video: Efficient Video Generation with Block Linear Diffusion Transformer
synopsis: SANA-Video addresses the prohibitive costs and slow speeds of existing video generation models that struggle to produce long, high-resolution content efficiently. The paper introduces a compact diffusion model using linear attention to reduce computational complexity while maintaining a fixed memory footprint for extended video sequences. This design achieves high-quality 720p video generation in seconds with training expenses at just one percent of comparable large-scale projects, enabling practical deployment on consumer-grade hardware. These capabilities unlock faster content creation workflows for digital streaming and live production, making advanced AI video tools accessible for everyday users and businesses without massive infrastructure investments.

synopsis: SANA-Video addresses the prohibitive costs and slow speeds of existing video generation models that struggle to produce long, high-resolution content efficiently. The paper introduces a compact diffusion model using linear attention to reduce computational complexity while maintaining a fixed memory footprint for extended video sequences. This design achieves high-quality 720p video generation in seconds with training expenses at just one percent of comparable large-scale projects, enabling practical deployment on consumer-grade hardware. These capabilities unlock faster content creation workflows for digital streaming and live production, making advanced AI video tools accessible for everyday users and businesses without massive infrastructure investments.

name: Song Han
paper_title: Locality-aware Parallel Decoding for Efficient Autoregressive Image Generation
synopsis: This research tackles the high latency and memory bottlenecks inherent in traditional autoregressive image generation, which typically produces one image patch per step. The authors propose Locality-aware Parallel Decoding (LPD), a new framework that utilizes flexible parallel modeling and optimized scheduling to generate multiple tokens simultaneously while preserving image quality. By decoupling context representation from token generation, the method reduces total generation steps by over 90% on standard resolutions and achieves at least 3.4x lower latency compared to prior models without compromising fidelity. These advancements enable faster text-to-image synthesis and unlock practical capabilities like zero-shot editing and inpainting while ensuring compatibility with existing vision perception backbones for unified multimodal systems.

name: Wuyang Li
paper_title: Stable Video Infinity: Infinite-Length Video Generation with Error Recycling
synopsis: Current AI video generation tools are limited to short clips because quality degrades over time due to accumulated errors during the creation process. Stable Video Infinity addresses this by employing Error-Recycling Fine-Tuning, which trains models to actively identify and correct their own mistakes by recycling previous errors as supervision signals. This approach enables the generation of ultra-long, non-looping videos without adding computational costs during use while remaining compatible with diverse controls like audio and text. Consequently, this technology unlocks new possibilities for creating hour-scale presentations and dynamic short-form content that supports frequent scene changes beyond the homogeneous scenes typical of existing long-video methods.

name: Zhao Yang
paper_title: Extending Sequence Length is Not All You Need: Effective Integration of Multimodal Signals for Gene Expression Prediction
synopsis: This paper challenges the prevailing belief that predicting gene expression requires processing extremely long DNA sequences, finding that such approaches often degrade performance due to model limitations and background noise. Instead, the authors introduce Prism, a framework that leverages nearby multimodal signals while using causal techniques to correct for background noise patterns that mislead standard models. Their results demonstrate that this method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy with significantly shorter sequences, bypassing the computational cost and technical constraints of modeling massive genomic ranges. Consequently, researchers can develop more efficient tools for personalized therapy strategies and understanding disease pathogenesis without relying on complex long-sequence architectures.

name: Yuanchen Ju
paper_title: MomaGraph: State-Aware Unified Scene Graphs with Vision-Language Models for Embodied Task Planning
synopsis: This research tackles the challenge of enabling household robots to navigate and manipulate objects effectively by creating a better way for them to understand their dynamic environment. The authors introduce MomaGraph, a unified scene representation that combines spatial layouts with functional relationships and part-level details, along with a large-scale dataset and new evaluation benchmark to ensure robust performance. Their trained model achieves top-tier accuracy among open-source tools while successfully planning tasks on physical robots without needing prior specific training for every situation. Ultimately, this advancement enables more reliable smart home automation systems that can execute complex daily chores safely and adaptively.

name: Junlin Han
paper_title: Learning to See Before Seeing: Demystifying LLM Visual Priors from Language Pre-training
synopsis: This research explores how Large Language Models gain hidden visual knowledge from text alone, addressing the opportunity to build efficient multimodal AI without expensive image data. The authors broke down this visual capability into separate perception and reasoning skills, showing that reasoning comes from code and math training while perception develops from general internet content. Their main result is that these two abilities scale differently depending on the specific data sources used during pre-training. This discovery gives developers a practical strategy to create strong multimodal models by strategically mixing text data, significantly reducing costs and time required for next-generation AI systems.

name: Bartłomiej Marek
institution: CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security
paper_title: Benchmarking Empirical Privacy Protection for Adaptations of Large Language Models
synopsis: Differential privacy theory often fails to protect private data in adapted large language models because pretraining overlaps undermine security guarantees. To address this gap, researchers benchmarked various adaptation methods using advanced attacks to measure real-world leakage risks across different data distributions. They discovered that models handling similar data to their pretraining suffer higher privacy risks even without direct overlap, whereas parameter-efficient methods like LoRA offer better protection for unrelated tasks. These findings provide developers with actionable guidelines for securely deploying customized AI in sensitive sectors like healthcare or finance.

paper_title: Benchmarking Empirical Privacy Protection for Adaptations of Large Language Models
synopsis: Differential privacy theory often fails to protect private data in adapted large language models because pretraining overlaps undermine security guarantees. To address this gap, researchers benchmarked various adaptation methods using advanced attacks to measure real-world leakage risks across different data distributions. They discovered that models handling similar data to their pretraining suffer higher privacy risks even without direct overlap, whereas parameter-efficient methods like LoRA offer better protection for unrelated tasks. These findings provide developers with actionable guidelines for securely deploying customized AI in sensitive sectors like healthcare or finance.

name: Zhaoyang Liu
institution: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
paper_title: ScaleCUA: Scaling Open-Source Computer Use Agents with Cross-Platform Data
synopsis: This paper addresses the scarcity of specialized training data that limits the ability to build autonomous computer-use agents capable of operating across different software platforms. To overcome this, the authors created ScaleCUA by utilizing a hybrid pipeline that combines automated agent exploration with human expert annotations to generate a massive, cross-platform dataset spanning Windows, macOS, iOS, and web environments. Trained on this data, their models achieve new state-of-the-art results in task completion and interface understanding while enabling seamless interaction without relying on closed proprietary APIs. This research unlocks significant potential for developing scalable automation tools that can efficiently manage tasks across diverse enterprise infrastructure with reduced data collection costs.

name: Zehao Li
institution: University of Science and Technology of China
paper_title: Half-order Fine-Tuning for Diffusion Model: A Recursive Likelihood Ratio Optimizer
synopsis: Current methods for fine-tuning diffusion models struggle with high memory costs or unstable training, often causing the models to collapse into noise. This paper proposes a new optimization technique called Recursive Likelihood Ratio that enables efficient gradient estimation using a unique half-order method to avoid these pitfalls. Experiments demonstrate that this approach significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on both image and video generation tasks while requiring less computational power. Ultimately, this breakthrough unlocks scalable ways for developers to reliably adapt high-fidelity generative AI models for specific commercial applications in resource-constrained environments.

name: Guanhua Zhang
institution: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems, Max-Planck Institute
paper_title: Train-before-Test Harmonizes Language Model Rankings
synopsis: Existing language model benchmarks often yield conflicting rankings for similar skills, creating confusion in model selection and obscuring true model potential. To resolve this, the researchers introduce a 'train-before-test' framework where every model is given identical benchmark-specific fine-tuning before evaluation to neutralize pre-training data biases. This method harmonizes rankings across 24 different benchmarks and demonstrates that inherent model quality follows a single dominant factor rather than varying task performances. Consequently, developers and investors can use this approach to reliably compare model capabilities for downstream tasks, ensuring they select models with genuine potential rather than those merely optimized for specific test patterns.

name: Guanjun Wang
institution: University of Science and Technology of China
paper_title: One for Two: A Unified Framework for Imbalanced Graph Classification via Dynamic Balanced Prototype
synopsis: Graph Neural Networks often struggle with real-world data because it is frequently skewed toward common classes and large graphs, causing models to ignore rare but important instances. To fix this, the authors developed UniImb, a unified framework that uses dynamic balanced prototypes to ensure both minority classes and small-scale graphs receive equal attention during training. Extensive testing on 19 datasets, including a new air pollution dataset, shows the method consistently outperforms 23 existing baselines while maintaining efficiency. This breakthrough allows industries in environmental monitoring, molecular science, and network security to build more accurate AI models that do not overlook rare, high-value patterns.

name: Hao Tan
institution: Institute of automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
paper_title: Veritas: Generalizable Deepfake Detection via Pattern-Aware Reasoning
synopsis: Existing deepfake detectors often fail in real-world scenarios because they rely on narrow training data that doesn't reflect evolving forgery techniques or diverse image domains. This paper addresses that gap by introducing HydraFake, a robust dataset, and VERITAS, a multi-modal model equipped with pattern-aware reasoning to analyze images like human forensic experts. Experiments show the system significantly outperforms current state-of-the-art methods on unseen content while delivering transparent explanations for its decisions. Consequently, this research unlocks safer digital environments by enabling reliable detection of sophisticated AI-generated threats on social media and video platforms.

name: Seonggyun Lee
paper_title: Semi-Supervised Preference Optimization with Limited Feedback
synopsis: This research addresses the high costs associated with collecting human-labeled preference data needed to align large language models. The authors propose Semi-Supervised Preference Optimization, which combines a small set of labeled feedback with large-scale unlabeled datasets by using a learned reward threshold to generate pseudo-labels. Their method effectively distills useful preference signals from existing text pairs while reducing reliance on expensive expert annotation. Experiments show the technique significantly outperforms traditional baselines trained on ten times more data, unlocking cost-efficient pathways for scaling safe and aligned AI models.

name: Sungjun Lim
paper_title: Semi-Supervised Preference Optimization with Limited Feedback
synopsis: This research addresses the high costs associated with collecting human-labeled preference data needed to align large language models. The authors propose Semi-Supervised Preference Optimization, which combines a small set of labeled feedback with large-scale unlabeled datasets by using a learned reward threshold to generate pseudo-labels. Their method effectively distills useful preference signals from existing text pairs while reducing reliance on expensive expert annotation. Experiments show the technique significantly outperforms traditional baselines trained on ten times more data, unlocking cost-efficient pathways for scaling safe and aligned AI models.

name: Zhuoyang Zhang
paper_title: Locality-aware Parallel Decoding for Efficient Autoregressive Image Generation
synopsis: This research tackles the high latency and memory bottlenecks inherent in traditional autoregressive image generation, which typically produces one image patch per step. The authors propose Locality-aware Parallel Decoding (LPD), a new framework that utilizes flexible parallel modeling and optimized scheduling to generate multiple tokens simultaneously while preserving image quality. By decoupling context representation from token generation, the method reduces total generation steps by over 90% on standard resolutions and achieves at least 3.4x lower latency compared to prior models without compromising fidelity. These advancements enable faster text-to-image synthesis and unlock practical capabilities like zero-shot editing and inpainting while ensuring compatibility with existing vision perception backbones for unified multimodal systems.

name: Shijie Zhao
paper_title: Reasoning as Representation: Rethinking Visual Reinforcement Learning in Image Quality Assessment
synopsis: Current top-performing image quality assessment models rely on slow, resource-heavy reasoning processes that limit their use in real-time scenarios like mobile devices or live video monitoring. The authors identify that reinforcement learning enables these models to convert redundant visual data into compact text representations that drive generalization. Building on this insight, they propose RALI, a framework using contrastive learning to align images directly with these representations without requiring reasoning steps or loading large LLMs during inference. This solution matches top-tier accuracy while reducing memory and time usage by more than 95%, unlocking efficient deployment for mobile devices, live video platforms, and generative AI workflows.

name: Tao Ren
paper_title: Half-order Fine-Tuning for Diffusion Model: A Recursive Likelihood Ratio Optimizer
synopsis: Current methods for fine-tuning diffusion models struggle with high memory costs or unstable training, often causing the models to collapse into noise. This paper proposes a new optimization technique called Recursive Likelihood Ratio that enables efficient gradient estimation using a unique half-order method to avoid these pitfalls. Experiments demonstrate that this approach significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on both image and video generation tasks while requiring less computational power. Ultimately, this breakthrough unlocks scalable ways for developers to reliably adapt high-fidelity generative AI models for specific commercial applications in resource-constrained environments.

name: Zehao Li
paper_title: ScaleCUA: Scaling Open-Source Computer Use Agents with Cross-Platform Data
synopsis: This paper addresses the scarcity of specialized training data that limits the ability to build autonomous computer-use agents capable of operating across different software platforms. To overcome this, the authors created ScaleCUA by utilizing a hybrid pipeline that combines automated agent exploration with human expert annotations to generate a massive, cross-platform dataset spanning Windows, macOS, iOS, and web environments. Trained on this data, their models achieve new state-of-the-art results in task completion and interface understanding while enabling seamless interaction without relying on closed proprietary APIs. This research unlocks significant potential for developing scalable automation tools that can efficiently manage tasks across diverse enterprise infrastructure with reduced data collection costs.
